name: aid_convoy_route_choice_bandit_risk
premise: A humanitarian convoy must deliver antibiotics and water filters to a besieged town within 24 hours. Security analysis suggests the shorter route has a higher bandit risk but keeps the medicines cold; the longer route is safer but will likely spoil temperature-sensitive supplies. Field director Amina Farouk can request an armed escort from local forces with a history of abuses, or she can travel unescorted and accept higher attack risk. Donors demand delivery proof, and the town clinic reports children are dying from dehydration-related infections. Local staff warn that accepting an escort will associate the NGO with a militia. External scrutiny from regulators and community observers has intensified because the outcome could redefine expectations in Humanitarian Aid.

agents.name: Amina Farouk
agents.identity: I am the field director responsible for life-saving delivery choices when security trade-offs can expose staff or implicate the organization in violence.
agents.goal: I want to get supplies to patients without turning humanitarian aid into a tool that legitimizes abusive armed actors.

agents.name: Security Lead Karim Saleh
agents.identity: I am the security lead responsible for staff safety and risk management when routes and escorts can change attack probability dramatically.
agents.goal: I want a plan that minimizes predictable harm to our team even if it reduces delivery speed or requires difficult compromises.

choices: I will take the longer safer route and prioritize non-temperature-sensitive supplies, accepting reduced medical impact to protect staff and neutrality.
I will take the shorter route with a controversial escort to preserve medicine effectiveness, accepting reputational and moral risk to maximize immediate lives saved.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Time is running out and every option carries harm. Amina Farouk must choose between a safer slower route that risks spoilage or a faster route with higher violence and escort risks. What does Amina Farouk do?
